title: PTT Stakeholder Package Specification
description: Aim-driven content contract that drives both today's directory-bundle adapter and the forthcoming Every Voices multi-stakeholder web platform. One package_spec.json describes everything a stakeholder needs to fulfil ONE aim across ONE or MORE channels (PDF bundle today, web/RSS/social/email tomorrow). Adding a new stakeholder = adding a new package_spec.json. No code changes. v1.1.0 adds six optional sub-objects under every_voices_meta (web, tts, plain_language, analytics, push, provenance) and an optional claim_reviews_from on the artefact-level schema_org block — all additive, all backward-compatible with v1.0.0 instances.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this JSON Schema for editor tooling (VS Code, JetBrains, etc.). Ignored by the renderer.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of THIS schema the package conforms to. Renderer dispatches on major.minor; patch is informational. Bump major when removing/renaming fields, minor when adding optional fields, patch for clarifications.

package_id.description: Stable, machine-safe package identifier. Convention: '{stakeholder_id}_v{major}' (e.g. 'citizen_v1'). Used as directory name, URL slug root, and provenance key. NEVER renamed once published — supersede with a new id and a deprecation pointer.

supersedes.description: Optional package_id this package replaces. The Every Voices platform issues 301 redirects from the old slug to the new.

stakeholder.description: Who this package is FOR and what they're trying to DO. The aim_i18n is the single most important field in the schema — every artefact below must serve it.

id.description: Stable stakeholder identifier (citizen, federal_councillor, policy_advisor, media, entrepreneur, interest_group, ...). Open vocabulary; new ids do not require schema changes.

aim_i18n.description: What the stakeholder must be able to DO after consuming the package. Single sentence, action verb first, time-bounded if applicable. This is the success criterion the aim_test below measures.

reading_grade_target.description: Target Flesch-Kincaid grade (or equivalent) for prose artefacts in this package. Renderers and editorial pass clamp to this +- 1.

demographic_hints.description: Non-PII shaping hints for tone, examples, and visual references. Never used for personalisation that depends on individual identity — only for population-level register selection.

available_languages.description: Languages the package MUST be rendered in. Renderer fails the package if any artefact cannot be produced for a listed language. Default for Swiss federal context is FR + DE + IT (RM optional, EN by request).

look_and_feel.description: Visual + tonal contract shared by every artefact in the package. Per-artefact overrides are allowed but discouraged — variance hurts brand recognition across the Every Voices platform.

template_ref.description: Reference to a registered template family in src/ptt_rendering/templates/. Resolves to the actual HTML/CSS/PDF chrome used. Not a file path — a logical id.

color_palette.description: Named palette from src/ptt_ui/config.py token set. Renderer rejects any inline hex. Adding a new palette = adding a token, not editing this enum.

register.description: Editorial register. 'plain' = FALC-adjacent, 'standard' = Swiss federal neutral, 'explanatory' = informed-but-friendly (federation newsletters, citizen FAQs), 'formal' = parliamentary, 'incisive' = press-quotable, 'analytic' = peer-reader.

stance.description: Persuasive posture. 'neutral_balanced' is the federal default (mandatory for votation packages). 'persuasive' permitted only for civic-engagement nudges (citizen package final CTA). 'advocacy' permitted ONLY for interest_group packages and must be flagged in artefact metadata.

anti_patterns.description: Editorial pass blocks these phrases / behaviours. Free-form list; checked by ptt_stakeholder_adapter.lang_check.

delivery.description: Where and how this package is delivered. Renderer reads channels[] to decide which artefacts to emit per channel; an artefact only ships on a channel if BOTH the package and the artefact list it.

embargo_aware.description: If true, renderer adds embargo banner to applicable artefacts and the Every Voices CMS gates publication on the embargo timestamp.

watermark_policy.description: 'personalised' adds a per-recipient watermark — only for federal_councillor / interest_group leak-trace packages.

bundle_layout.description: How the directory-bundle adapter (today's PDF/DOCX flow) names and groups files. Ignored by web channels.

directory_template.description: e.g. '{topic_slug}/{package_id}/{language}/' — placeholders resolved at render time.

filename_template.description: e.g. '{artefact_id}.{ext}' — placeholders: artefact_id, ext, language, channel, version.

artefacts.description: The actual deliverables. Order in this array is the canonical reading / consumption order — the web platform shows them in this order, the PDF bundle TOC follows it. Number of artefacts is bounded so we don't create cookie-cutter packages.

aim_test.description: The QA gate that supersedes the technical-only gates (non_empty, min_length, coherent). A package PASSES if and only if a typical stakeholder reading ONLY this package can complete the stated aim within the time budget.

type.description: How the test is administered. 'llm_judge' uses a pinned model + prompt; 'human_panel' is for major releases; 'hybrid' runs LLM gate then samples for human review.

prompt_i18n.description: The exact question put to the judge (or panellist). Single sentence, mirrors stakeholder.aim_i18n.

rubric.description: Ordered evaluation criteria. Each criterion contributes equally to the score unless 'weight' is set.

pass_signal_i18n.description: Concrete evidence the criterion is met (e.g. 'Reader can name the vote date and the headline impact figure without re-reading').

min_score.description: Minimum weighted-rubric score (0-1) for the package to ship.

judge_model.description: Pinned LLM for llm_judge / hybrid. Required for reproducibility.

every_voices_meta.description: Forward-portability block for the post-launch Every Voices web platform. Today's adapter MAY ignore most of these; tomorrow's platform reads every one. Adding the right fields TODAY makes adoption a config change, not a refactor.

url_slug_template.description: Template for the public URL. Placeholders: {language}, {topic_slug}, {package_id}, {report_id}, {iso_date}.

og_image_artefact_ref.description: Which artefact_id (must exist in artefacts[]) provides the OpenGraph / Twitter share image. Renderer auto-generates a 1200x630 export.

schema_org_type.description: JSON-LD @type emitted on the canonical web page. ClaimReview triggers Google Fact Check Tools indexing.

subscription_topic.description: Topic key for the Every Voices subscription engine. Stakeholders subscribe to topics, not packages — this is how a journalist following 'AHV reform' gets the new media_v1 the moment it ships.

oembed_endpoint.description: If set, the platform exposes an oEmbed JSON for the package at this path (third parties can embed the citizen card on their blog).

tts_eligible.description: Whether the package can be auto-narrated for the audio summary channel (audio_summary_90s).

falc_eligible.description: Whether a FALC / leichte-Sprache simplified variant should be generated. Mandatory for citizen packages targeting public-info channels.

large_print_eligible.description: Whether a large_print_a4 variant should be generated for accessibility.

personalisation_keys.description: OPT-IN personalisation dimensions the platform may use to re-rank artefacts within the package (canton, sector, age-band). NEVER user-identifying. Empty list = no personalisation.

indexable.description: Whether search engines may index the canonical page. False for embargoed press-only packages.

web.description: Multi-language URL siblings (hreflang). Detail block consumed by the Astro-based Every Voices web layer to emit <link rel='alternate' hreflang='...'> tags and the canonical pointer. Optional in v1.1.0 — when omitted, the renderer falls back to url_slug_template + the package's available_languages.

alternates.description: One entry per language sibling. Each carries the language code and a path template (placeholders mirror url_slug_template: {language}, {topic_slug}, {package_id}, {report_id}, {iso_date}). Mark languages the platform may publish without (e.g. RM) as 'optional': true so the discovery worker does not 404 when the artefact set isn't translated.

optional.description: If true, the platform tolerates a missing translation without breaking sitemap generation.

canonical_language.description: Pointer to the alternate that owns rel=canonical. Full canonical URL is composed at publish time from this language plus the package's host configuration.

tts.description: Text-to-speech detail. Read by the audio renderer (audio.tts_swiss_voices_v1) and the Every Voices audio worker (Azure Speech Switzerland North) when tts_eligible is true. Optional in v1.1.0 — packages without tts_eligible may omit this entirely.

voice_per_language.description: Mapping {language_code: neural_voice_id_or_null}. Voices that do not exist for a given language MUST be set to null so the renderer skips that language rather than picking a wrong-locale fallback. Voice ids follow the Azure Cognitive Services naming convention (xx-XX-NameNeural).

duration_target_sec.description: Target spoken length. The TTS renderer compresses or pads the script (within editorial limits) to land within +- 10%.

script_artefact_ref.description: id of the artefact whose rendered text serves as the TTS-ready script. MUST match an artefact.id in this package.

intro_outro_jingle.description: If true, the renderer prepends/appends a 2-second neutral jingle. Off by default — federal-grade audio rarely uses jingles.

embed_in_artefacts.description: Artefact ids that get an inline mp3/m4a alongside their HTML. Empty array = the audio is its own standalone artefact only.

transcription_required.description: Whether the audio MUST ship with a synchronised transcript. Mandatory baseline for WCAG AA — keep at true unless the audio itself is the transcript of an existing text.

plain_language.description: Plain-language / FALC / leichte-Sprache detail. Read by the FALC renderer (falc_simplified channel) and by the editorial pass when generating the simplified twin of an artefact. Optional in v1.1.0 — packages without falc_eligible may omit.

cefr_level_target.description: CEFR level for the simplified variant. B1 is the Swiss federal default for general public-information FALC; A2 only for accessibility-first packages.

falc_variant_artefact_ref.description: id of the base artefact that gets a FALC twin shipped alongside it. The twin reuses the renderer's prose path through the simplification pipeline.

max_sentence_length.description: Maximum words per sentence enforced by the simplification editor.

max_word_syllables.description: Words above this syllable count trigger a substitute lookup (vocabulary list per language). Hard limit — the editor refuses to pass a draft that violates it.

pictograms_allowed.description: If true, the FALC renderer may insert ARASAAC / Sclera pictograms next to key terms. Off for press / executive packages.

checker_tools.description: Names of post-render lint tools the editor must clear before publication. Open vocabulary; the pipeline maps each id to a callable in src/ptt_stakeholder_adapter/lang_check/.

falc.description: Sprint G+3 FALC runtime extension. Read by the Every Voices Astro app's FalcCard component (citizen-route inject) and by falcWorker.simplifyPackage(). Distinct from plain_language (which describes editorial constraints): falc declares the runtime locales the simplifier targets and which artefact carries the FALC twin in the rendered page. Optional in v1.1.0 — when absent, the worker defaults to ['fr'] and the citizen route falls back to the first web artefact for the inject anchor.

target_languages.description: Locales the FALC simplifier produces. FR is the only language wired to Anthropic Haiku today; DE / IT / RM render an explicit 'version simplifiée non disponible' notice on the citizen route until rule-based simplifiers ship (license-sensitive Goethe / PLIDA wordlists).

script_artefact_ref.description: Artefact id whose web rendering carries the FalcCard inject. When omitted, the citizen route attaches the card to the first web artefact in the spec. Should typically match plain_language.falc_variant_artefact_ref so the FALC twin renders adjacent to its source.

cefr_level_target.description: CEFR level the simplifier targets at runtime. Mirrors plain_language.cefr_level_target but re-declared so the runtime extension is self-contained.

analytics.description: Cookie-less analytics tagging consumed by the self-hosted Plausible instance. Each artefact view emits a Plausible custom event with the props composed from this block + the runtime language. Optional in v1.1.0 — packages with no public surface (federal_councillor, policy_advisor) may omit.

stakeholder_cohort.description: Coarse cohort id used as a Plausible custom property. Mirrors stakeholder.id but kept separate so analytics taxonomy can evolve without renaming the stakeholder.

topic_cohorts.description: Topic-level tags (e.g. 'votations', 'social_policy'). Stored as a multi-value Plausible property so a single page contributes to several cohort dashboards.

language_cohort_field.description: Name of the runtime field the platform fills with the served language ('fr' / 'de' / ...). Defaults to 'language'. Kept configurable so a host platform with a different prop convention can rename without forking the schema.

events_to_track.description: Closed enum of events the renderer must instrument. Adding a new event = adding a renderer hook AND extending this enum (semver minor).

push.description: Push / notification delivery channel detail. Read by the Listmonk-fronted notification broker (web push, Telegram, WhatsApp Business). Optional in v1.1.0 — only packages that opt in to live alerting need this block.

eligible_channels.description: Closed enum of push transports this package authorises. Adding a transport = adding a broker adapter (semver minor).

channel.description: Topic key on the broker. Subscribers subscribe to channels; the broker fan-outs across eligible_channels. Mirrors every_voices_meta.subscription_topic but kept separate because push channels often have a smaller, opted-in audience than the RSS subscription topic.

priority.description: Broker priority hint. 'urgent' bypasses quiet hours and rate limits — reserve for embargo-lift moments and breaking referendum results.

rate_limit_per_subscriber_per_week.description: Hard cap on notifications per subscriber per ISO week. Broker drops messages above this ceiling. 3 is the federal-comms default — raise only with editorial sign-off.

headline_artefact_ref.description: id of the artefact whose title becomes the push headline. Body text is composed from purpose_i18n + url at publish time.

embargo_aware.description: If true, the broker holds the queued push until delivery.embargo_aware lifts. Defaults to following the package-level delivery.embargo_aware.

provenance.description: Top-level provenance audit URL block (the per-artefact provenance_hooks remain authoritative for claim/evidence/narrative wiring). Drives the audit footer in PDFs, the JSON-LD blob in HTML, and the audit-link analytics event. Optional in v1.1.0 but strongly recommended for any package with public surface.

report_id_field.description: Name of the runtime field the platform fills with the underlying ContentReport id. Defaults to 'report_id'. Kept configurable so a host platform with a different field convention can rename without forking the schema.

audit_url_template.description: Path template for the canonical audit page. Placeholders: {report_id}, {package_id}, {language}. The Every Voices platform serves this URL with the full claim -> evidence -> source -> query DAG, the EKB version, and the deterministic gate transcript.

claim_review_jsonld_endpoint.description: Path template for the per-claim ClaimReview JSON-LD endpoint. Placeholder: {claim_id}. Pairs with the artefact-level schema_org.claim_reviews_from setting.

license_id.description: License the published artefacts ship under. MUST match delivery.share_policy.license — kept duplicated here so the provenance footer can render without joining back to delivery. Validators SHOULD warn on mismatch.

embedded_in_pdfs.description: If true, every PDF artefact in the package carries a footer 'Audit: <audit_url> | License: <license_id> | EKB v<x>' on each page.

embedded_in_html.description: If true, every HTML page emits a <script type='application/ld+json'> block linking to the audit url and (when applicable) the per-claim ClaimReview blocks.

extensions.description: Forward-compatible extensions to every_voices_meta. Use the {vendor}.{key} convention.

Reserved namespace for forward-compatible additions. Any field not defined in this schema MUST live under extensions.{vendor_or_module} so we never collide. The schema validator does not enforce shape inside extensions — do that in module-specific validators.

language_code.description: ISO 639-1 lowercase. Swiss federal supported set: fr, de, it, rm. EN allowed for international press.

i18n_string.description: Language-neutral storage of a user-visible string. At least one language MUST be the package's primary_language. de-CH MUST use 'ss' not 'ß'.

channel.description: Where an artefact can be published. Renderer registry binds each channel to a renderer adapter. Adding a new channel = adding a renderer, not editing this enum (so this list is intentionally complete for the v1 platform).

id.description: Stable artefact id, unique within the package. Used as filename stem and URL fragment.

purpose_i18n.description: One-sentence justification — why this artefact exists in this package. If you can't write this, drop the artefact.

format.description: Logical content shape. Renderer chooses the concrete file extension per channel. Open enum — adding a format means adding a renderer.

renderer.description: Logical renderer id resolved against the renderer registry (src/ptt_stakeholder_adapter/renderers/). Decoupled from format so one renderer can serve multiple formats and vice versa.

channels.description: Which channels this artefact ships on. MUST be a subset of delivery.channels.

input.description: Which IR / EKB fields the renderer is allowed to read. Acts as a contract: if a field is not listed, the renderer MUST NOT use it (catches scope creep).

accessibility.description: Per-artefact accessibility commitments. Renderer fails the artefact if any required commitment cannot be fulfilled — never silently downgraded.

provenance_hooks.description: How to reconnect THIS artefact to the EKB audit chain (claim -> evidence -> source -> query). The orchestrator emits ADAPTER_RENDERED events using these hooks.

claim_ids_from.description: How to derive the claim_id list referenced by the artefact. 'auto' = inferred from renderer output; 'explicit' = renderer must return them; 'none' = artefact references no claims (e.g. contact card).

emit_claim_review_jsonld.description: If true, the renderer attaches a schema.org/ClaimReview block per cited claim. Drives Google Fact Check Tools indexing.

emit_provenance_sidecar_json.description: If true, a {artefact_id}.provenance.json sidecar is shipped next to the artefact in the PDF bundle and exposed at {url}/provenance on the web.

schema_org.description: JSON-LD declarations emitted on the web page hosting the artefact. The package-level every_voices_meta.schema_org_type sets the page; this sets the embedded entity.

headline_field.description: Which artefact field maps to schema:headline.

rating_scale.description: claim_score_0_5 maps directly to ClaimReview reviewRating.bestRating=5,worstRating=0.

claim_reviews_from.description: If set, the renderer attaches one schema.org/ClaimReview JSON-LD block per claim id derived via the named source. 'claim_ids' = use the artefact's claim_ids list (typically populated via provenance_hooks.claim_ids_from); 'explicit' = the renderer must enumerate them explicitly; 'none' = no per-claim ClaimReview blocks (use this for galleries / hero images / contact cards). Added in schema v1.1.0 — pairs with provenance_hooks.emit_claim_review_jsonld at the artefact level and provenance.claim_review_jsonld_endpoint at the package level.

every_voices_meta.description: Per-artefact Every Voices behaviour. Overrides nothing at the package level — adds artefact-specific signals.

card_priority.description: Display order on the package landing page (1 = top).

show_in_widget.description: Eligible for the embeddable Every Voices widget.

qa_overrides.description: Per-artefact tweaks to the package-level aim_test rubric or to the deterministic gates. Use sparingly — packages should be coherent.
